title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 9: Problem Solving
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 24 + 37 - 19?
How many triangles can you count in the figure shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 10,70 110,70'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle split by a vertical line from its apex to the base, creating two smaller triangles inside.
questions.solution: The two smaller triangles formed by the vertical line plus the large outer triangle makes 3 triangles total.

questions.question_text: Lucas starts his homework at 4:20 pm and finishes 45 minutes later. What time does he finish?
questions.solution: 4:20 pm plus 45 minutes equals 5:05 pm.

questions.question_text: Emma has two 10-dollar bills, three 5-dollar bills, and four 1-dollar coins. How much money does she have?
What number comes next in the pattern: 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ___?
questions.solution: Each number increases by 3, so 14 + 3 = 17.

questions.question_text: Which shape shown has exactly four equal sides and four right angles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='15'/><rect x='50' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='40' height='20'/><polygon points='160,15 185,30 160,45 135,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: From left to right: a circle, a square, a rectangle, and a rhombus.
questions.solution: A square has four equal sides and four right angles. A rhombus has equal sides but not right angles; a rectangle has right angles but not equal sides.

questions.question_text: Ben is taller than Carla. Carla is taller than Dave. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: Ben > Carla > Dave, so Dave is the shortest.

questions.question_text: A ribbon is 1 meter long. Kate cuts off 35 centimeters. How many centimeters of ribbon are left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='10'/><line x1='73' y1='5' x2='73' y2='25'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A horizontal ribbon with a short vertical tick mark showing a cut point.
questions.solution: 1 meter equals 100 cm. 100 - 35 = 65 cm.

questions.question_text: A box holds 6 eggs. How many boxes are needed to pack 50 eggs so that none are left out?
questions.solution: 8 boxes hold 48 eggs, leaving 2 eggs. A 9th box is needed for the remaining 2 eggs.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are shaded in the grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid of squares in a checkerboard pattern with corners and center shaded.
questions.solution: The four corners plus the center square are shaded, giving 5 shaded squares.

questions.question_text: Oliver eats one half of a pizza and his sister eats one quarter of the same pizza. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='30'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='70' y2='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into a left half and two quarters on the right.
questions.solution: Together they eat 1/2 + 1/4 = 3/4. The leftover is 1 - 3/4 = 1/4.

questions.question_text: There are 5 friends at a table and each one shakes hands with every other friend exactly once. How many handshakes happen in total?
questions.solution: Each pair shakes hands once: 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 10 handshakes.

questions.question_text: In a sequence, every term is made by adding the two numbers before it. If the sequence starts 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, what is the next term?
questions.solution: Add the last two terms: 5 + 8 = 13.

questions.question_text: A notebook costs 7 dollars and a pencil costs 2 dollars. Mia buys 3 notebooks and 4 pencils. How much does she pay?
questions.solution: 3 x 7 = 21 for notebooks, 4 x 2 = 8 for pencils. Total = 21 + 8 = 29 dollars.

questions.question_text: A train leaves the station at 9:40 am and arrives at 12:15 pm. How long is the trip?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='30'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='15'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='55' y2='45'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A simple clock face with an hour hand and a minute hand.
questions.solution: From 9:40 am to 12:40 pm is 3 hours. Subtract 25 minutes to get 12:15 pm, so the trip is 2 hours 35 minutes.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has a length of 8 cm and a width of 5 cm. What is the perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle that is wider than it is tall.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2 x (8 + 5) = 2 x 13 = 26 cm.

questions.question_text: Four children sit in a row. Anna is not at either end. Ben is to the left of Anna. Carla is at the right end. Who sits at the left end?
questions.solution: Carla is rightmost. Anna is in the middle. Ben is left of Anna, so Ben is in a middle seat. That leaves Dan at the left end.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 36. What is the largest of the three numbers?
questions.solution: Divide 36 by 3 to get the middle number 12. The numbers are 11, 12, 13, so the largest is 13.

questions.question_text: A larger square is made from 9 identical small squares. If the side of each small square is 4 cm, what is the perimeter of the larger square?
questions.image_description: A big square divided by internal lines into a 3 by 3 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: Each side of the large square is 3 x 4 = 12 cm. Perimeter = 4 x 12 = 48 cm.

questions.question_text: Peter places coins in a row following a rule: red, red, blue, red, red, blue, and so on. What color is the 20th coin?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='15' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='15' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='15' r='10' fill='currentColor'/><circle cx='90' cy='15' r='10'/><circle cx='115' cy='15' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='15' r='10' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of circles with the 3rd and 6th circles filled, showing the repeating pattern.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 coins. 20 divided by 3 gives remainder 2, so the 20th coin matches the 2nd in the pattern, which is red.

questions.question_text: A water jug holds 2 liters. Sam pours out 350 milliliters three times. How many milliliters of water remain in the jug?
questions.solution: 2 liters = 2000 mL. Poured out: 3 x 350 = 1050 mL. Remaining: 2000 - 1050 = 950 mL.

questions.question_text: In a basket there are only apples and oranges. There are 15 fruits in total, and the number of apples is twice the number of oranges. How many apples are there?
questions.solution: Let oranges = x, apples = 2x. Then 3x = 15 so x = 5. Apples = 2 x 5 = 10.

questions.question_text: Each face of a cube is painted. The cube is then cut into 8 equal smaller cubes. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly 3 painted faces?
questions.image_description: A cube drawn with faint lines showing it is divided into 8 equal smaller cubes.
questions.solution: When a painted cube is split into 8 smaller cubes, each of the 8 corner cubes has exactly 3 painted faces.

questions.question_text: A number is multiplied by 4, then 6 is added. The result is 34. What was the original number?
questions.solution: Work backwards: 34 - 6 = 28, then 28 divided by 4 = 7.
